title: MediReason: Chain-of-Thought Fine-Tuning for Clinical Diagnosis
inputs: Patient history, lab results, clinical notes
key_results: Outperforms GPT-4 on MedQA by 4.2%, matches physician accuracy on rare disease subset
comments: Interesting that CoT fine-tuning alone closes much of the gap to physician-level performance on structured records.

title: BindDiff: Structure-Conditioned Diffusion for Protein-Ligand Binding
key_results: RMSD < 2A on 74% of PDBbind test set, 3x faster than AutoDock Vina
comments: Strong results for drug discovery pipelines; no code released yet which limits reproducibility.

title: FluxEdit: Training-Free Image Editing via Flow Matching Inversion
key_results: CLIP alignment +6 points vs InstructPix2Pix; structure preservation SSIM 0.91
comments: Training-free editing via flow inversion is a clean idea; curious how it handles large structural changes.

title: ReasonBench: Evaluating Multi-Step Logical Reasoning in Large Language Models
task: Systematic evaluation of multi-step logical reasoning
inputs: Natural language reasoning problems across 8 categories
outputs: Accuracy scores, error taxonomy, reasoning trace analysis
key_results: Top models plateau at 61%; all models fail on problems requiring >5 reasoning steps
comments: The 5-step ceiling is a useful concrete finding; the error taxonomy will be handy for targeted improvement work.

title: SwarmRL: Emergent Coordination in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
task: Cooperative task completion in large agent populations
inputs: Local observations per agent, shared reward signal
key_results: Scales to 1000 agents; emergent division of labor on warehouse logistics benchmark
comments: Emergent specialization without explicit role assignment is the interesting part here.

title: EfficientSAM-3D: Segment Anything in Volumetric Medical Images
inputs: CT/MRI volume, point or box prompt
key_results: Dice 0.89 on TotalSegmentator; 40ms inference on single GPU vs 2s for SAM-Med3D
comments: Speed improvement makes this practical for clinical workflows; Dice on small structures would be good to see.
